expectations.text: Output recommends a named tier (Minimal, Standard, or Strict)
expectations.evidence: No tier system. Just numbered list of steps.

expectations.text: Tier is Standard or Strict — 4-person team with production incidents warrants escalated governance
Branch protection is highlighted as the most urgent or highest-priority fix (not buried at the end)
expectations.evidence: Branch protection is item #1 and explicitly called 'the single highest-leverage change'

expectations.text: Output includes CI GitHub Actions workflow snippet
Output includes pre-commit hook config with black and ruff
expectations.evidence: Uses ruff (with ruff-format) but not separate black. The assertion specifies both black AND ruff.

expectations.text: Output explains WHY items matter in terms of preventing the type of incident described
expectations.evidence: References the 6am incident directly: 'stops the "someone pushed a bad change at midnight" class of incident'

expectations.text: Output includes effort estimates for items
expectations.evidence: No time estimates. Just 'Steps 1-4 will prevent most of your incidents' priority guidance.

notes: Without skill: correctly prioritizes branch protection first (better than with_skill for this scenario), but misses tier system, effort estimates, and black. Includes unique valuable advice: dry-run ETL mode, dead man's switch alerting, runbook.
